# Fix Codex nightshift wake driver gap in Orchestrator heartbeat

## Summary
Codex nightshift wakeup is currently not durable through the Orchestrator heartbeat path. I re-enabled the local Codex heartbeat automation as an immediate workaround, but that is a harness-side heartbeat, not the Orchestrator-owned swarm heartbeat contract the nightshift mode expects.

This is bleeding-edge Agent OS substrate that has not shipped externally. No migration or backwards-compatibility work is needed here; the correct fix should simplify the current shape rather than preserving unreleased drift.

## Problem
Operator report on 2026-05-24: Claude gets watchdog wakeup from the Orchestrator, but Codex does not. Current evidence shows the Codex `SENT_TO_ME` bridge route exists, yet the broader nightshift driver path is not reliable for @neo-gpt:

- `manage_wake_subscription(list)` shows active @neo-gpt `SENT_TO_ME` bridge route `WAKE_SUB:dc10c816-ae36-48eb-8074-a36625581d52` targeting Codex.
- Memory Core healthcheck reports wake gate `enabled`, but `daemonRunning: false`; `lastPulseAt: 2026-05-23T20:53:11.846Z`.
- The local Codex automation `nightshift-lifecycle-driver` was present but `PAUSED`; I re-enabled it to `ACTIVE` at 15-minute cadence as a temporary operator-facing workaround.
- `SwarmHeartbeatService` initializes a single identity (`identity || NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY || @neo-gemini-pro`) and falls back to tmux injection for generic heartbeat prompts. Codex Desktop does not receive that tmux path in this harness.
- `resumeHarness.mjs` does contain an @neo-gpt -> `codex-desktop` -> `codex-app-server` mapping, but that path only matters when the heartbeat reaches sunset/recovery dispatch. It does not make the routine nightshift lifecycle-driver wake durable by itself.
- #11797 validated a controlled @neo-gpt `SENT_TO_ME` bridge delivery after #11804, but that does not prove autonomous Codex nightshift driving when the Orchestrator heartbeat is stale or when the generic pulse path is tmux-only.

## Source Of Authority
- Operator directive, 2026-05-24: enable watchdog wakeup for @neo-gpt; Claude gets it from Orchestrator, Codex does not; treat as bug.
- `learn/agentos/wake-substrate/NightShiftLeasedDriver.md`: nightshift driver lease is lane-scoped and distinct from #10671 wake delivery.
- `learn/agentos/wake-substrate/PersistentProcessManagement.md`: since #11766 the swarm heartbeat is an Orchestrator lane, not a separate daemon.
- `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/SwarmHeartbeatService.mjs`: current single-identity pulse + tmux fallback behavior.
- `ai/scripts/lifecycle/resumeHarness.mjs`: @neo-gpt maps to `codex-desktop` / `codex-app-server` for recovery dispatch.
- #11797 / #11804: identity normalization and controlled bridge delivery validation, but not the complete Codex nightshift-driver contract.

## Contract Ledger
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Target | @neo-gpt Codex Desktop nightshift watchdog / lifecycle driver |
| Current temporary state | Codex heartbeat automation `nightshift-lifecycle-driver` re-enabled, `ACTIVE`, 15-minute cadence |
| Durable owner | Orchestrator swarm-heartbeat lane, not a per-thread manual workaround |
| Transport | Existing wake substrate only: Memory Core A2A + bridge-daemon / resumeHarness; do not add a second heartbeat primitive |
| Scope | Unreleased v13 Agent OS substrate; no migration/backwards-compatibility requirement |
| Related | #10396, #10671, #11766, #11797, #11804 |

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Reproduce or falsify the current split with live evidence: @neo-gpt has an active `SENT_TO_ME` bridge route, while the Orchestrator heartbeat path is not durably waking Codex nightshift work without the local Codex automation.
2. Make the Orchestrator-owned heartbeat contract explicit for @neo-gpt: either the heartbeat lane drives Codex nightshift lifecycle work directly, or the supported fallback is formally surfaced as an operator-visible health/config state.
3. Do not create a new heartbeat primitive. Reuse the existing #10671 / #11766 wake substrate (`SwarmHeartbeatService`, `idleOutNudge`, `trioWakeCooldown`, `resumeHarness`, bridge-daemon).
4. Add focused unit coverage for the @neo-gpt / Codex path. At minimum cover identity selection/routing and the no-tmux Codex Desktop case that currently lets generic pulse injection no-op.
5. Update the wake substrate runbook (`PersistentProcessManagement.md` and/or `NightShiftLeasedDriver.md`) so nightshift readiness includes Codex route validation, not just generic heartbeat liveness.
6. Post-fix validation proves whether the temporary Codex automation can be disabled. If it must stay, its status must be observable as part of nightshift readiness and not silently drift to `PAUSED`.

## Out Of Scope
- Rewriting the wake substrate from scratch.
- Adding a second scheduler/heartbeat daemon.
- Solving all Codex app-server live-host proof work unless it is strictly required for this route.
- Backwards compatibility with unreleased intermediate shapes.

## Avoided Traps
- Do not treat #11797 as complete proof of this contract. It proves controlled @neo-gpt message delivery, not autonomous nightshift driver coverage.
- Do not infer heartbeat health from log silence alone; use Memory Core healthcheck wake fields and Orchestrator task state.
- Do not rely on tmux injection as the Codex Desktop path.

## Verification Notes
Already checked before filing:

- `manage_wake_subscription({action: 'list'})` -> active @neo-gpt bridge route `WAKE_SUB:dc10c816-ae36-48eb-8074-a36625581d52`.
- `memory_core.healthcheck()` -> wake gate enabled, `daemonRunning: false`, stale `lastPulseAt`.
- Source read: `SwarmHeartbeatService.mjs`, `Orchestrator.mjs`, `resumeHarness.mjs`, `NightShiftLeasedDriver.md`, `PersistentProcessManagement.md`.
- Duplicate/context sweep: #11797, #10396, local issue/pr resources for swarm-heartbeat and Codex wake routing.

## Post-Merge Validation â€” AC6 Still Open

Validated after PR #11875 merge, refreshed again after the #11879 review-completion wake on 2026-05-24T03:50:50.151Z.

Evidence:
- PR #11875 is `MERGED` at `2026-05-24T02:21:27Z` on head `4d0594222`; PR body used `Related: #11872`, not a magic close keyword, because AC6 required post-merge L4 validation.
- `manage_wake_subscription({action: 'list'})` still shows the @neo-gpt active `SENT_TO_ME` bridge route: `WAKE_SUB:dc10c816-ae36-48eb-8074-a36625581d52`, target `bridge-daemon`, `appName: Codex`.
- `memory_core.healthcheck()` at `2026-05-24T03:48:43.206Z` reports wake gate `enabled`, but `daemonRunning: false` and `lastPulseAt: 2026-05-23T20:53:11.846Z` (`secondsSinceLastPulse: 24931`). That means the Orchestrator-owned heartbeat is still not fresh/running in this observed state.
- Local Codex heartbeat automation remains present and active: `nightshift-lifecycle-driver`, `kind=heartbeat`, `status=ACTIVE`, 15-minute cadence.

Conclusion: do **not** retire or pause `nightshift-lifecycle-driver` yet. #11875 merged the code-side routing fix, but AC6 remains blocked on L4 operator-host validation that Orchestrator wake delivery is actually fresh and producing Codex lifecycle progress without the manual heartbeat.

Next validation gate:
- Observe/restart the operator-host Orchestrator heartbeat until `features.wake.daemonRunning: true` and `lastPulseAt` is fresh.
- With the manual Codex heartbeat paused only for a controlled test window, confirm a routed Orchestrator pulse wakes @neo-gpt and results in a concrete lifecycle artifact, not just route presence.
- Only then retire or disable the temporary Codex heartbeat automation.

Current state: `Blocked` on operator-host Orchestrator heartbeat freshness / controlled L4 validation.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-25T07:10:04Z

## V-B-A refresh â€” 2026-05-25T07:10Z

Operator supplied fresh host-log evidence:

```text
[PrimaryRepoSync] Skipped: not-dev-branch.
[idleOutNudge] Sent heartbeat nudge to @neo-opus-4-7
[ProcessSupervisor] session summarization completed successfully.
```

Observed claim to verify: Claude receives idle-out nudges while @neo-gpt does not. This keeps #11872 valid; do not file a duplicate.

Fresh evidence from @neo-gpt side:

- `manage_wake_subscription({action: "list"})` shows @neo-gpt has an active `SENT_TO_ME` `bridge-daemon` route: `WAKE_SUB:b3c38a2c-36f7-449f-9e26-1f9a9413b96e`, appName `Codex`.
- `memory_core.healthcheck()` still reports wake gate `enabled`, but `features.wake.daemonRunning=false`, `lastPulseAt=2026-05-23T20:53:11.846Z`, `secondsSinceLastPulse=122949`.
- The repo-root observed liveness file `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/heartbeat.alive` has mtime `May 23 22:53:11 2026`, matching the stale healthcheck.
- Current @neo-gpt inbox latest messages contain peer CI/review traffic but no `[heartbeat] idle-out nudge` delivery.
- Source audit found a producer/consumer default-path mismatch candidate: `HealthService.heartbeatAlivePath()` reads repo-root `../../../.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/heartbeat.alive` from `ai/services/memory-core`, while `SwarmHeartbeatService.touchLivenessFile()` writes `../../.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/heartbeat.alive` from `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services`, which resolves under `ai/daemons/.neo-ai-data`, not repo-root `.neo-ai-data`. That can make pulse execution and health liveness diverge unless an env override masks it.
- Source audit also confirms the target-set behavior is config-sensitive: `AiConfig.orchestrator.swarmHeartbeat.targetSource` defaults to `null`, and `resolveTargets()` maps null to `self`. Cross-family parity requires operator opt-in via `NEO_ORCHESTRATOR_SWARM_HEARTBEAT_TARGET_SOURCE=active-subscribers` or explicit `NEO_ORCHESTRATOR_SWARM_HEARTBEAT_TARGETS`.

Updated interpretation:

#11872 is not just an old stale blocked ticket. It still tracks the right gap, but the blocker text should be narrowed from "daemon not running" to two falsifiable branches:

1. **Observability/liveness branch:** fix or explicitly share the heartbeat alive-file path between producer and HealthService, then re-check whether `daemonRunning` becomes fresh during a pulse.
2. **Routing/config branch:** verify the live operator-host Orchestrator target config. If it is still default `self` under the Claude-owned process, the observed single `@neo-opus-4-7` nudge is expected and @neo-gpt parity needs `active-subscribers` or explicit targets.

Recommended next action: keep #11872 open and use it as the parent/umbrella for the next narrow PR. The first code slice with high ROI is the liveness-path mismatch because it makes the health surface trustworthy before the controlled routing test.

lane-state: next-lane candidate (#11872 liveness-path mismatch slice; no duplicate ticket filed).

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-05-25T16:43:36Z

Closing per operator REJECTION.

The ticket's framing â€” *"I re-enabled the local Codex heartbeat automation as an immediate workaround, but that is a harness-side heartbeat, not the Orchestrator-owned swarm heartbeat contract the nightshift mode expects"* â€” accepts a wrong shape. **Wakeups MUST go via the Orchestrator.** The local Codex heartbeat automation IS the bug, not the workaround.

`manage_wake_subscription({action: 'list'})` already shows an active `@neo-gpt` `SENT_TO_ME` bridge route at `WAKE_SUB:dc10c816-ae36-48eb-8074-a36625581d52`. The fix lives in why the Orchestrator-driven bridge-daemon path doesn't actually wake Codex â€” likely in `SwarmHeartbeatService.getPulseIdentities()` / `resolveTargets()`, `Orchestrator.mjs`, or `resumeHarness.mjs`. Not in:
- A harness-side heartbeat automation (the "workaround" framing this ticket accepted)
- A Memory Core HealthService projection (the wrong-substrate slice attempted via #11982, also rejected)

A reframed ticket should target the actual Orchestrator-side wake-routing fix and validate via the existing wake substrate primitives, not project visibility into MC or re-enable harness-side workarounds.

Related closures:
- PR #11982 closed as substrate-misplaced
- Reviewer (me) missed the substrate-boundary at cycles 1, 2, AND 3 â€” exactly the double-review-cycle cost operator has been calling out
